title: State Conference Day 1 — Check-In & Opening Session
location: Marriott Denver Tech Center, 4900 S Syracuse St, Denver CO
description: Day 1 of the Colorado FBLA State Leadership Conference. Members check in, attend the opening ceremony with keynote speaker, and participate in competitive event preliminaries. Business casual attire required. Bring two printed copies of all required materials.
notes: Room 214 for Mobile App Dev check-in. Bring laptop + phone for demos.

title: State Conference Day 2 — Competitive Events
location: Marriott Denver Tech Center, 4900 S Syracuse St, Denver CO
description: Day 2 of the Colorado FBLA State Conference — the main competition day. All competitive event presentations and finals take place today. Mobile Application Development presentations are scheduled 9:00 AM–3:00 PM in Ballroom C. Professional business attire required. This is the day that defines your State experience.
notes: PRESENTATION DAY — Mobile App Dev slot: 10:30 AM, Room C-4. Arrive 15 min early.

title: State Conference Day 3 — Awards Ceremony
location: Marriott Denver Tech Center — Grand Ballroom
description: Closing day of the Colorado FBLA State Conference featuring the Awards Ceremony and closing session. NLC qualifiers will be announced. Professional attire required. Celebrate your chapter's accomplishments and connect with FBLA members from across Colorado.
notes: Awards start at 10:00 AM sharp. Check-out by 12:00 PM.

title: Mobile App Dev Practice Presentation
description: Chapter practice presentation session for all Mobile Application Development competitors. Each student presents their app for 7 minutes followed by 3 minutes of Q&A from peers and Ms. Rivera. Feedback forms provided. Dress as you would for State.
notes: Bring device with app loaded and backup APK on USB drive.

title: District 12 Chapter Meeting
description: Monthly District 12 FBLA chapter meeting. Agenda: State Conference logistics, fundraiser recap, officer elections for next year, and committee updates. All members encouraged to attend. Light refreshments provided.

title: FBLA Membership Dues Deadline
description: Final deadline to pay FBLA membership dues for the 2025–2026 school year. Unpaid members will not be eligible to compete at State. Dues are $20 national + $10 Colorado state chapter. Pay through the FBLA member portal or submit cash/check to Ms. Rivera.

title: State Conference Registration Closes
description: Last day to register for the Colorado FBLA State Leadership Conference. Late registrations will not be accepted. Ensure all competitive event entries are submitted, hotel rooms are booked through the conference block, and permission slips are signed.

title: NLC Registration Opens
description: National Leadership Conference 2026 registration opens. NLC will be held June 24–27, 2026 in Atlanta, Georgia. Only State Conference qualifiers (top 3 in each event) are eligible to register for NLC. Registration fee: $75 per member plus travel and hotel.
notes: Register immediately if you qualify at State!

title: NLC Registration Deadline
description: Final deadline for National Leadership Conference 2026 registration. All NLC-qualifying members must be registered and fees paid by this date. Contact Ms. Rivera for assistance with paperwork.

title: National Leadership Conference — Atlanta 2026
location: Georgia World Congress Center, Atlanta, GA
description: The 2026 FBLA National Leadership Conference in Atlanta, Georgia — June 24–27. The pinnacle of FBLA competitive events. Top competitors from every state compete for national recognition. Over 12,000 FBLA members attend NLC annually. Formal business attire required for all competitive events.

title: Chapter Fundraiser — Bake Sale
location: Cherry Creek High School — Main Lobby
description: Annual FBLA chapter bake sale fundraiser to fund State Conference travel and registration costs. All members encouraged to bring baked goods and volunteer for 1-hour shifts during school hours. Goal: raise $500 for the chapter travel fund.

title: District Leadership Conference
description: District 12 Leadership Conference — a practice competition and networking event for all District 12 FBLA members. Top finishers in competitive events qualify for State Conference. Business professional attire required. Great opportunity to meet FBLA members from other District 12 schools.
notes: Placed 1st in Mobile App Dev — qualified for State! 🏆

title: Chapter Coffee Hour & Debrief
description: Post-State social gathering to celebrate results from the State Leadership Conference. Members share highlights, debrief on competitive events, and enjoy coffee and snacks together. A relaxed, celebratory wrap-up after a hard-fought State Conference.

title: NLC Prep Meeting
description: Planning session for NLC qualifiers heading to Atlanta in June. Agenda covers travel logistics, hotel assignments, competition prep timeline, and fundraising needs. All State qualifiers are required to attend; other members welcome.

title: Senior FBLA Farewell Dinner
location: Cheesecake Factory Cherry Creek, 3000 E 1st Ave, Denver CO
description: Annual dinner to honor Cherry Creek FBLA seniors graduating this spring. Current members celebrate the seniors' contributions to the chapter and wish them well. Speeches from officers and advisors; slideshow of the year's highlights.

title: End-of-Year Officer Elections
description: Annual chapter officer elections to select leadership for the 2026–2027 school year. Candidates for President, VP, Secretary, Treasurer, and committee chairs will present brief speeches. All current members in good standing are eligible to vote.

title: NLC Travel Meeting
description: Final logistics meeting for the Atlanta NLC trip. Covers flight details, hotel check-in procedures, competition schedules, per diem, and packing guidelines. All NLC-attending members and their parents/guardians are strongly encouraged to attend.

title: Summer FBLA Community Service Day
location: Denver Food Bank, 2875 N Havana St, Denver CO
description: Chapter community service volunteer event at the Denver Food Bank. Members sort and package food donations to support families in need across the metro area. Counts toward the FBLA Community Service Award and chapter service hours.

title: New Member Orientation
description: Welcome event for students joining FBLA for the first time. Covers chapter history, competitive events overview, membership benefits, and how to get involved. Current officers lead the presentation; light refreshments served afterward.

title: Chapter Kickoff Meeting
description: First full chapter meeting of the 2026–2027 school year. New officers present their goals, committees are formed, and the year's event calendar is shared. All returning and new members are expected to attend.

title: Business Etiquette Workshop
description: Guest speaker from a local Denver business presents on professional etiquette, networking skills, and workplace communication. Interactive Q&A and mock networking activity included. Directly applicable to FBLA competitive events like Business Communication and Job Interview.

title: Competitive Event Selection Deadline
description: Deadline for all chapter members to select their competitive events for the 2026–2027 year. Submit selections through the FBLA member portal and notify Ms. Rivera of your choices. Members who miss this deadline may not be eligible to compete at District.

title: Fall Chapter Meeting
description: Q1 check-in meeting covering progress on competitive event preparation, fundraising updates, and upcoming conference deadlines. Committee chairs report on their areas. Great time to ask questions and connect with chapter leadership.

title: Mock Interview Practice
location: Cherry Creek High School — Career Center
description: Structured mock interview practice session to prepare members competing in Job Interview, Business Communication, and presentation-based events. Volunteer business professionals from the community serve as interviewers. Dress professionally.

title: Scholarship Application Workshop
description: Workshop to help members identify and apply for FBLA scholarships with upcoming deadlines. Ms. Rivera walks through the application process, essay tips, and required materials. Seniors especially encouraged to attend.

title: FBLA Membership Dues Open
description: Membership dues for the 2026–2027 FBLA year are now open. Dues are $20 national + $10 Colorado state chapter and must be paid to remain eligible for competitive events. Pay through the FBLA member portal or submit to Ms. Rivera.

title: Winter Social & Awards
description: Semester wrap-up celebration recognizing member achievements, presenting chapter awards, and kicking off winter break. Food, music, and a slideshow of the fall semester. All members and their families are welcome to attend.

title: District 12 Winter Conference
description: First competition of the 2026–2027 year for District 12 FBLA members. Top finishers qualify to advance to the State Leadership Conference in spring. Business professional attire required. A great benchmark for competitive event readiness.
